comments.text: 1. The statement ''Gyárfás and Lehel [GyLe95] proved \( \tfrac12<c<\tfrac35\)'' is slightly imprecise. More precisely, [GyLe95] implies \(\frac12+\frac{1}{141\cdot 76} \le c \le \frac35\). 2. Ma and I have just posted a paper (arXiv:2602.23282) in which we improve the known bounds on the constant \(c\), obtaining\[\frac{9}{17}\le c \le \frac{4}{7}.\]This work made exploratory use of ChatGPT-5.2 Pro, with all mathematical arguments subsequently checked by the human authors. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
This is now constant 5b in the optimization constant database.
Standard check by GPT finds no issues.
Is the assumption $A \subset \mathbb{R}_{>0}$ important? I don't think in [GyLe95] they assume that every element of $A$ is positive. Maybe I am missing sth.

No (since the problem is invariant under translation, so you can assume without loss of generality that $A\subset \mathbb{R}_{>0}$). In fact you can also assume, without loss of generality, that $A\subset \mathbb{N}$.
